name: Red-Lipped Batfish
primary_sources: Fishes of the World (Nelson, Grande, Wilson, 2016)

visual_attributes.morphology: flattened body with a disk-like shape and a pointed rostrum on the snout
distinctive_features: large lateral eyes positioned high on the head
visual_attributes.texture: shagreen-like with a relatively smooth texture
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: up to 40 centimeters (16 inches) in length
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: pectoral, pelvic, and anal fins adapted for walking; dorsal fin as a lure

domain_specific_visual.FIN_ADAPTATION: pectoral, pelvic, and anal fins function like limbs for walking
domain_specific_visual.LIP_COLORATION: bright red lips for species recognition

domain_specific_functional.COURTSHIP_BEHAVIOR: distinctive dance by males to attract females
domain_specific_functional.EGG_GUARDING: males guard and fan eggs to maintain oxygen flow

relationships.description: closely related species found near Cocos Island
native habitat of the red-lipped batfish

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Pacific Ocean around the Galápagos Islands and off Peru
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: symbol of unique marine biodiversity in the Galápagos
primary_sources: Fishes of the World (Nelson, Grande, Wilson, 2016)

contrastive_constraints: NOT a deep-sea fish without walking fins
